concept: blacklevel / bilevel cutoff: threshold choice preserves silhouette
source_excerpt: double blacklevel; /* 0 to 1: black/white cutoff in input file */   ...   int bilevel;        /* convert to bilevel? */   double level;       /* cutoff grey level */
implication: The grey cutoff that decides which pixels become foreground directly controls how much of the silhouette survives. A too-high cutoff eats thin/light features (breaks silhouettes into speckle); a too-low cutoff floods the image with background. Choosing the cutoff at the foreground/background luminance valley preserves the silhouette while rejecting noise.

param_mapping.rationale: vtracer in color mode has no exposed blacklevel; this is a pre-tracer binarization/threshold choice (analogue of potrace blacklevel/mkbitmap level, range 0-1, default 0.5). Relevant when a source is pre-binarized before tracing, or for a binary colormode path. INFERRED pipeline knob. The vectorizer's foreground detection (lum<240 | sat>25) is the current implicit cutoff; this records blacklevel as the tunable behind it.

hypothesis: Setting the pre-trace grey cutoff at the luminance histogram valley (near 0.5, source-dependent) rather than a fixed default will preserve thin silhouette features and reduce edge speckle, improving edge_iou, because it separates foreground from background where they actually divide.
